sheet.bio:
Overview

Name: Valkera Blackheart (Née Goldenthal) 

Race: Human 

Gender: Female 

Spouse: Thorin Blackheart 

Age: 36 

Culture: Thaian 

Birth: Unknown Height: 1,72m 

Personality: Diligent, arrogant, irritable

Defining Traits

Feeble Frame — This character was born delicate and is often sick and bruises easily. Blackheart In-law — This character married into the House of Blackheart.

Exalted Devotion — This character has dedicated their life to the service of a higher power.

Background

Born among the gutters of Thais, Valkera survived her childhood as a thief and pickpocket. Even then, strange healing powers stirred within her — powers she later learned to channel through runecraft. For a time she earned coin beside the trader Sarra Zoldan, carving healing runes for the Followers of the White Dragon in Cormaya, until betrayal and exploitation forced her to flee by ship to Liberty Bay.

There, fortune abandoned her once more. Her business was ruined, her charity repaid with suspicion and fear. After tending to diseased children rejected by corrupt priests, Valkera was branded a cultist by an angry mob and driven from the city.

Exile led her to the Mountain Hideout and the Circle of Golgari. Among wandering mystics and scholars she found stability for the first time in her life. The sorcerer Pertwee taught her to read and write, while the Circle opened her eyes to forgotten lore and hidden powers. Yet peace did not last.

Drawn into the Dark Side civil war through her companion Equoez, Valkera spent years as a battle mage in the Bey faction, witnessing cruelty, death, and the slow erosion of her ideals. Disillusioned, she deserted and vanished into the Fields of Glory before eventually resurfacing on the island of Senja. There she lived quietly as a fisherwoman until fate drew her back toward civilization through her friendship with the elf Othelen.

Together they travelled to Fibula, where Valkera joined the Red Rose. Within the Red Rose she rebuilt herself. She gained a formal education in stewardship, established a successful trading house in Liberty Bay, and married into the noble House of Blackheart. In time she rose to the Council as Magistra Noviciorum.

But peace proved fleeting once again. When both Othelen and Equoez disappeared, Valkera uncovered a conspiracy aimed at destroying the Red Rose from within. Though the plot was stopped, her friends were lost. Othelen was slain by the ancient Paladin of Darkness known as Osamiron, while Equoez vanished beyond death itself.

Haunted by failure and driven by hatred for corruption, Valkera abandoned trade and politics alike. Together with her companion Roderic Rivershaw, she reclaimed the abandoned Mountain Hideout and unearthed its ancient and powerful secrets. From these foundations they forged the Order of the Argent Gryphon, devoted to wisdom, strength, and the destruction of evil.

Under Valkera's leadership, the Argent Gryphon rapidly grew in influence. Alliances were forged, initiates recruited, and old cults hunted beneath the mountains of Vandura. Yet the deeper the Order delved into buried secrets, the darker the discoveries became.

Then came disaster. While Valkera negotiated a defensive pact with Fibula, Roderic fell by a terrible curse. Though still alive, his mind had become distant and unreachable. Valkera halted the Order's campaigns entirely, abandoning plans for war in favour of a desperate search for a cure.

Now, as Lady Warden of the Argent Gryphon, Valkera stands at a crossroads. What are honour, duty, and justice worth beside the warmth of friendship and brotherhood? And how far will she descend to save those she loves?

Roderic Rivershaw was born in the quiet hamlet of Rosebud, where he grew up among the farmers and fishers. He grew lanky and strong hauling carts and chopping timber for the local blacksmith, his sinewy frame tempered by honest labour. A jagged scar arcs across his left cheek – an echo of a childhood brawl when he defended his master's son from a local bully. His hair is dark as raven’s wing, cropped close except for a singlAe plait down the nape, braided in remembrance of his late mentor. His grey‐steel eyes burn with quiet determination, and when he moves, there’s the steady rhythm of someone who’s spent years aligning body and purpose.

When the trumpets of the Order of the Argent Gryphon sounded through Rosebud, Roderic felt a stirring in his bones. Though he wears the Order’s gleaming breastplate now – an argent gryphon alighting on a field of red – he never forgets the simple leather jerkin beneath it. He greets every noble and peasant alike with the same courtesy, believing that honour is not a gift of birth but a choice of the heart.

Physical Traits

Towering build
Just shy of six foot four, muscular without ever seeming overburdened
Notable features
The scar along his left cheek; a single braided lock of hair with a carved wooden bead
Eyes
Steely grey, level and thoughtful, quick to soften when faced with suffering

Character Traits

Resolute
Once he commits, nothing – neither fear nor fatigue – can sway his will
Compassionate
Instinctively draws to those in need, believing true strength is measured by what one protects
Humble
Wears his knightly mantle lightly, always mindful of the humble hearth where he first learned swordcraft

Character Hooks

Family Legacy
Rumours whisper that his bloodline hides a dormant nobility - will he pursue his ancient heritage or forsake it for his newfound future in the Order
Inner Conflict
Haunted by the moment he drew blood in defence of his cousin, Roderic struggles with the morality of violence even as he stands sworn to martial service.
Old Oath
Before joining the Argent Gryphon, Roderic swore a blood-oath to a dying blacksmith whose life he couldn’t save. Now, the memory of that promise drives him to protect the weak – he fears that one day he may be called upon to fulfill the oath in a way that conflicts with his duty as a knight.

Valarya is a human knight of the Argent Gryphon bearing the rank of Oathsworn, though whispers place his name among the remnants of the ancient Order of the Black Knights — an order long buried beneath myth, denial, and deliberate forgetting.

He is not a man who appears cruel at first meeting. Valarya carries himself with restraint uncommon among knights. He speaks little unless spoken to directly, listens with unsettling attentiveness, and rarely wastes emotion on displays of anger, pride, or zeal. Most remember him first as calm. Reasonable. Measured. The sort of man capable of standing unmoved while others lose themselves to fear or outrage.

That stillness is what draws people toward him. He possesses a talent for understanding what others need to hear, particularly in moments of uncertainty. To the doubtful, he offers certainty. To the ashamed, absolution. To the ambitious, purpose. He does not manipulate through charm so much as through permission — an ability to make people feel their worst instincts are not weaknesses, but necessary truths they were too frightened to admit aloud.

He believes deeply that every institution eventually rots beneath the weight of its own ideals. Honour becomes vanity. Faith becomes control. Mercy becomes weakness exploited by the merciless. Where others react to this decay with outrage or despair, Valarya accepts it with near religious calm. In his view, corruption is not a failure of mankind, but its most honest state.

Despite this, he is neither reckless nor openly sadistic. Violence is a tool he employs without hesitation, but rarely without purpose. He dislikes spectacle, distrusts fanaticism, and views emotional cruelty as wasteful indulgence. What unsettles others most is not brutality, but the absence of visible hatred behind it. Valarya can condemn a man with the same composure another knight might use to discuss the weather.

Among the Argent Gryphon, his influence is difficult to measure because it rarely appears forceful. Decisions made around him simply tend to drift toward harsher conclusions. Compromises become ultimatums. Necessary evils become accepted practice. Over time, men who once considered themselves virtuous find themselves defending acts they would previously have condemned, often without remembering precisely when their thinking changed.

Whether this is deliberate manipulation or merely the consequence of Valarya's presence is unclear. Even his loyalties remain uncertain. Some believe the vows he carries as Oathsworn bind him utterly to the Argent Gryphon. Others suspect those oaths conceal an older allegiance to the forgotten doctrines of the Black Knights. Valarya himself has never clarified the matter, and those few who pressed him on it rarely found the answers comforting.

He has the unnerving habit of speaking about ruin the same way other men speak about winter — not with excitement, nor fear, but with quiet acceptance of something inevitable.
